title: Wait wait What about the Tree of Life
display_title: Wait wait What about the Tree of Life
wikitext:
[]: Wait, wait... What about the Tree of Life?             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Oct 06, 2007 7:49 pm It took me until just now to notice that the Classified Information mentions nothing about the Tree of Life. We know that the ToL is formed when Adam's Spear of Longinus penetrates the core of the (supposedly) Lilith-"born" Unit 01. She then goes undergoes apotheosis and, along with the Spear, becomes the ToL. It is referred to as the "propagule of souls", or "embryo of life", depending on how accurate you want to be. I feel like there's something to this that helps explain why Adam was pwned by his own Spear... if it had combined with Lilith, it would have made her even more powerful, according to EoE logic, right? But wait wait wait wait! How the crap does the Spear function as a weapon if it would turn a Source of Life into a god by combining with it?! This makes no sense to me. =( This does, however, offer one explanation: Adam's Spear of Longinus speared Adam as an act of self-sacrifice to prevent Third Impact (though it would have been Second back then), allowing the Lilim to survive, in the same way that Kaworu did. I mentioned that in a previous thread, but didn't have it straight enough in my head to elaborate that much. EDIT: Ooh! Okay, I think it makes sense now! The CI says that the Spear of Longinus is included with each Source of Life as a "security device" for safeguarding the FAR's plans. It does not ever refer to it as a weapon. Thus, its intention was probably to do just what it did: to bring down the Seed of Life it came with in the event that two Seeds land on the same planet, so as to prevent a Third Impact from occurring. Still, doesn't a Third Impact just make the other existing species the inheritor of the world? It would end up the same way, wouldn't it? Aw piddles, it still doesn't make sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sat Oct 06, 2007 10:11 pm The Black Moon is said to have landed near the South Pole, where Adam and the White Moon were already ensconced. Lilith's Spear is believed to have been lost at the time of Impact (thus Adam's Spear was the only one around). The proximity of the two Seeds of Life (one bearing the Fruit of Life, the other the Fruit of Knowledge) represented a danger of precisely the sort of eventuality that the Spears were intended by the FAR to prevent: the "forbidden union of Adam and Lilith" which would result in the Seeds attaining "god-status". Therefor, it was Adam's bad luck that, thru no fault of his own, he was the one that got put into suspended animation, since his Spear was intact, and available to act, and Lilith's was not. Seen from this perspective, the stuff about Lilith's spear being lost or destroyed, which seemed so lame at first, is actually a narrative necessity, since if her Spear had been around to take her out (which would be only fair, since she was crashing on a planet that legitimately belonged to Adam and his progeny), then where would that leave us ? No Lilith, no Lilim, no story! (and Adam's offspring would be living it up on this planet, happy as clams...)

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Oct 06, 2007 10:13 pm Hey, you're right! That does explain the whole Lilith's spear being lost thing... it's not unexplained, but it must have been destroyed. Finally, it's all been pieced together! Thanks for the help, you ol' broomstick! =D

[]:             Quote Post by ~at0mic punk~ » Sat Oct 06, 2007 10:17 pm Ok, so did the FAR actually intend for Lilith and Adam to be on the same planet or was it an accident?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Oct 06, 2007 10:19 pm ~at0mic punk~ wrote: Ok, so did the FAR actually intend for Lilith and Adam to be on the same planet or was it an accident?

[]:             Quote Post by ~at0mic punk~ » Sat Oct 06, 2007 10:24 pm NAveryW wrote: ~at0mic punk~ wrote: Ok, so did the FAR actually intend for Lilith and Adam to be on the same planet or was it an accident?

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Sun Oct 07, 2007 7:57 am I'm liking this backstory .... but is there any mention at all of the FAR in the actual TV series? Or is this all from the CI?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Oct 07, 2007 8:02 am CitizenGeek wrote: is there any mention at all of the FAR in the actual TV series?

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Sun Oct 07, 2007 8:09 am Mr. Tines wrote: CitizenGeek wrote: is there any mention at all of the FAR in the actual TV series?

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun Oct 07, 2007 8:27 am CitizenGeek wrote: Mr. Tines wrote: CitizenGeek wrote: is there any mention at all of the FAR in the actual TV series?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Oct 07, 2007 4:26 pm I still want to know, though: what does the FAR see wrong with a Third Impact that will destroy one of the Seeds if the Spear of Longinus would do the same?

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sun Oct 07, 2007 4:30 pm Merriam-Webster Online Dictionary wrote: Main Entry: Mac·Guf·fin Variant(s): or Mc·Guf·fin /m&-'g&-f&n/ Function: noun Etymology: coined by Alfred Hitchcock : an object, event, or character in a film or story that serves to set and keep the plot in motion despite usually lacking intrinsic importance

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Oct 07, 2007 4:44 pm I'm not convinced the Spear acted because of anything either Seed "did". There is a cryptic passage in the CI about a contest of survival. I can picture some fine print in their grand masterplan that if 2 of them end up on the same planet their offspring have to duke it out to see which version is better. Cruel, but it makes sense from an evolutionary standpoint.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun Oct 07, 2007 4:54 pm NAveryW wrote: I still want to know, though: what does the FAR see wrong with a Third Impact that will destroy one of the Seeds if the Spear of Longinus would do the same?
